title: Filesystem coordination substrate; git keeps history only, never locking
context: v1 used git commit+push races as the task-claim lock, while ARCHITECTURE.md declared .switchboard/ state gitignore-able — a contradiction (gitignored state cannot coordinate through git). Deployment target is a single dedicated machine.

options.name: git-as-lock
options.rationale: Claims are commit+push races; preserves a multi-machine/cloud-worker story immediately.
options.tradeoff: Seconds of latency per transition, a commit per claim polluting history, remote as SPOF, and orchestration state must stay git-tracked forever.

options.name: filesystem-lanes-with-git-history
options.rationale: Claims are atomic renames between lane directories plus a lease file; .switchboard/ is gitignored. Git keeps exactly two jobs: project source (branches/PRs) and the tracked decision spine.
options.tradeoff: Single-machine coordination only; a second machine needs a sync layer designed when it actually exists.

reasoning: Git-as-lock solved a distribution problem the deployment doesn't have, at real cost. Atomic renames + leases give instant claims and a structural infra-vs-task failure split (stale lease requeues, never fails). Explicit tracked/transient boundary: decisions/ (all ADR/AgDR/HDR/SDR records) and plans/ are committed and travel with the code; queue lanes, leases, in-flight results are transient. Invariant: a decision record may only cite evidence that survives .switchboard/ cleanup. Colin accepted single-machine scope at this stage. Amends HDR-003 (its gitignore instinct was correct once locking moved off git).
supersedes: README 'git is the lock' seam; amends HDR-003
